Scenario.title: Energetic Disruption: Water Curupira Pikabot Attack on Energy Sector

overview.description: The Water Curupira Pikabot campaign targets the energy sector through a multi-stage cyber attack, leveraging phishing, backdoor installation, and SCADA system manipulation to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Disruption of energy grid operations and potential sabotage of critical infrastructure.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-511: Identify Software/Services Used by Target
stage.description: Attackers send phishing emails to energy company employees, attaching malicious documents designed to mimic legitimate operational reports. These attachments exploit CVE-2022-27926, enabling remote code execution upon opening.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once initial access is achieved, attackers deploy malware on compromised servers. This backdoor leverages CVE-2021-21017 to gain persistence and escalate privileges, targeting misconfigured cloud storage services like exposed AWS S3 buckets.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Attackers exploit insecure APIs within the energy grid's SCADA systems. This allows them to manipulate data or issue unauthorized commands, potentially leading to power outages or physical damage to equipment.

Scenario.summary: The Water Curupira Pikabot campaign orchestrates a sophisticated attack against the energy sector, starting with phishing emails to gain initial access, followed by backdoor installation to establish persistence, and culminating in SCADA system manipulation to achieve maximum impact. This attack demonstrates the growing threat of cyber-physical attacks targeting critical infrastructure.
